definition: I2S substitutes multiple PNG structural bytes (chunk-type tags, IHDR fields, CRC/length) so the parser advances deep enough to allocate state (num_trans, palette, hist, splt) or set bitflags (PNG_FREE_TRNS/PLTE/HIST), enabling downstream state-presence checks.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes logged chunk-type FOURCC and structural comparison operands into the matching input offsets, manufacturing a valid tRNS chunk that causes libpng to allocate tRNS state and set PNG_FREE_TRNS in free_me, enabling the bitflag test to pass.
signature.one_line: I2S plants valid tRNS chunk bytes so PNG_FREE_TRNS bit gets set

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a well-formed tRNS chunk so libpng sets PNG_FREE_TRNS in free_me, satisfying the AND-mask gate.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid PNG whose chunk stream includes a parseable tRNS chunk (correct 'tRNS' FOURCC type tag plus a self-consistent length/CRC and a prior valid IHDR establishing color type) so that libpng allocates a tRNS entry and OR's the PNG_FREE_TRNS bit into info_ptr->free_me; the TRUE side of line 507 then becomes reachable.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner (cmplog) seeds share a stable, intact PNG header region (signature + IHDR + gAMA + sRGB) with concentrated byte values at the chunk-type/length offsets, indicating the parser advanced deep enough to recognize ancillary chunks and set free_me bits; the blocker TRUE side fires (W has T=11 at line 507 vs L T=0) and the W-only tRNS-free body (lines 508-513) executes.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser (naive) seeds corrupt exactly the header dataflow bytes that gate chunk parsing (e.g. IHDR dimension/color-type bytes at 0x16-0x19 and chunk-type bytes at 0x25-0x28 diverge from the winner's consistent values), so libpng never recognizes/allocates a tRNS entry, PNG_FREE_TRNS is never set in free_me, and line 507 stays on the FALSE side (L T=0 F=23).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer of intercepted integer/memory comparisons (chunk-type FOURCC checks, length/dimension comparisons libpng performs while parsing) and substitutes the expected operand directly into the matching input offsets, planting the exact magic/structural bytes needed for libpng to accept a tRNS chunk; naive must hit the same multi-byte constraints by blind havoc, which it never does in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked TRUE side gates on PNG_FREE_TRNS being set in info_ptr->free_me.
The true branch is the globally blocked side, and only the winner runs the tRNS-free body.
Winner takes the true side at line 507 while loser never does.
cmplog resolves all 10 trials while naive blocks all 10, isolating the I2S delta.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winner header/chunk bytes are concentrated and stable across seeds, indicating intact chunk parsing.
Loser seeds diverge from the winner at the IHDR dimension/color-type and chunk-type dataflow offsets.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets, which naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, only havoc/token operators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose tRNS-acceptance gate depends only on a single isolated byte comparison (not a multi-byte FOURCC/length structure) on which naive resolves the TRUE branch as fast as cmplog, showing I2S substitution is not what plants the required bytes.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The precise set of input offsets I2S must plant to make libpng allocate a tRNS entry is inferred from header-region byte stability, not from an explicit tRNS chunk visible in the shown 64-byte seed prefixes.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the logged hIST chunk-type literal and bit-constant operands into the matching input bytes, causing libpng to allocate hist state and set PNG_FREE_HIST in free_me, which value_profile cannot do through Hamming-distance feedback alone.
signature.one_line: I2S plants hIST chunk-type bytes to set PNG_FREE_HIST bitflag

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a valid hIST chunk so PNG_FREE_HIST is set in both mask and free_me, enabling the bitmask gate.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid hIST chunk (correct 4-byte chunk type plus dependency chunks like PLTE) so png reading allocates info_ptr->hist and sets the PNG_FREE_HIST bit in info_ptr->free_me; this makes ((mask & PNG_FREE_HIST) & info_ptr->free_me) non-zero at png.c:640.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are large (1013-1133 bytes) value_profile_cmplog inputs that drive deep into png_free_data, taking the hIST true branch (lines 641-645 marked [W]) and reaching PLTE/eXIf free paths the loser never hits; the branch row shows W T=6 F=23 versus L T=0 F=23 at line 640.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking value_profile seeds, though they share the same PNG/IHDR/gAMA/sRGB header bytes, never construct the chunk state that sets PNG_FREE_HIST; they spend their executions in earlier free paths (sCAL/pCAL lines 519-547 marked [L]) and at line 640 record T=0 F=23 — the true side is never taken across all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which value_profile lacks. I2SRandReplace substitutes logged comparison operands (the 4-byte hIST chunk-type literal and the bit constants compared while libpng dispatches chunk handlers) directly into the matching input bytes, letting the winner manufacture a valid hIST chunk that flips PNG_FREE_HIST. value_profile only nudges Hamming-distance feedback buckets and cannot place the exact chunk-type bytes, so it stalls.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is a bitmask AND gate over PNG_FREE_HIST and info_ptr->free_me whose true side is globally blocked.
The winner takes the hIST true branch and frees info_ptr->hist while the loser never does.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 6 times while the loser takes it zero times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 640  T=6 F=23  T=0 F=23  if (((mask & PNG_FREE_HIST) & info_ptr->free_me) != 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds are large value_profile_cmplog inputs (1013-1133 bytes).
Loser-blocking seeds come from value_profile and never resolve the branch across all 10 trials.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace whereas value_profile has no I2S mutator.
value_profile relies only on CMP_MAP distance buckets with no I2S substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a hIST-style 4-byte chunk-type literal where value_profile resolves the gate as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing CMP_MAP gradient alone suffices and I2S substitution is not the decisive technique.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I infer the exact chunk-type/bit literal that I2S substitutes from source structure rather than from a concentrated byte-diff offset directly aligning to the hIST chunk type, since the BYTE DIFF only shows header-region offsets.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes expected IHDR field values (dimensions, color-type, CRC) from the CmpLogObserver buffer into the matching input offsets, repairing the header so PNG parses far enough to allocate a palette and set PNG_FREE_PLTE in free_me.
signature.one_line: I2S repairs IHDR fields so PNG_FREE_PLTE bit gets set in free_me

analysis.summary_one_line: A structurally valid IHDR plus a parseable PLTE chunk so libpng internally allocates the palette and sets the PNG_FREE_PLTE free_me bit.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG header whose IHDR fields (width 0x5b, height 0x45, bit_depth 0x08, color_type 0x06) and chunk length/CRC bytes survive validation so that downstream PLTE-chunk handling internally allocates info_ptr->palette and sets the PNG_FREE_PLTE bit in info_ptr->free_me; only then does (mask & PNG_FREE_PLTE) & free_me evaluate non-zero (the blocked true side at png.c:649).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All 10 winner seeds carry an intact IHDR — offset 0x13 is 0x5b ([) in 10/10, 0x17 is 0x45 (E) in 10/10, 0x18 is 0x08 in 9/10, 0x1d is 0x52 (R) in 10/10, 0x1e is 0xed in 10/10 — yielding a consistent dimensions/color-type/CRC region that parses cleanly; the per-role overlay shows the true side at 649 is reached only in W (T=10 F=28 for W vs T=0 F=23 for L) and the palette-freeing body lines 650-654 are W-only.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds scatter those same header fields — offset 0x16 is a0/00/80/02, 0x17 is 86/00/45/03, 0x18 is 10/08/04/02, 0x1d is 7f/00/52/ff, 0x1e adds ff/00 — corrupting IHDR length/dimensions/CRC so the parse never reaches a valid PLTE allocation; at png.c:649 the loser only ever takes the false side (T=0 F=23), so info_ptr->palette is never internally allocated and PNG_FREE_PLTE stays clear in free_me.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer of operands from libpng's chunk-length/CRC/signature integer comparisons and substitutes the expected operand at the matching input offsets, repairing the IHDR/chunk fields so the PNG parses far enough to allocate the palette; naive has only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection, so it cannot reliably reconstruct the multi-field valid header and stays blocked in 10/10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a bitmask test of mask & PNG_FREE_PLTE against info_ptr->free_me whose true side is globally blocked.
The true (palette-freeing) side at 649 and its body are reached only by winner-resolving seeds.
At branch 649 the winner takes the true side ten times while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 649  T=10 F=28  T=0 F=23  if (((mask & PNG_FREE_PLTE) & info_ptr->free_me) != 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry a consistent intact IHDR field region.
Loser seeds scatter the IHDR dimension/length bytes that winners hold constant.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one logged CMP operand at offsets where the other operand matches.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A naive (no-I2S) trial reaching the true side at png.c:649 in a comparable fraction of trials, or a synthetic harness where corrupting only IHDR/chunk-length CMP-checked fields fails to gate cmplog's advantage over naive.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The mapping from input header bytes to the runtime info_ptr->free_me PNG_FREE_PLTE bit is inferred from source structure and per-role coverage, not directly traced, so I cannot prove the specific CMP whose operands I2S repaired.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes logged chunk-type/CRC comparison operands into matching input offsets, enabling deep chunk parsing that populates info_ptr arrays; the resulting valid chunk state makes num != -1 true when png_free_data is reached.
signature.one_line: I2S fixes chunk literals so deep parsing populates info arrays for num!=-1

analysis.summary_one_line: Inputs must carry valid multi-entry chunk structures (text/sPLT/unknown) so png_free_data is invoked with a per-item num != -1.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG with valid chunk-type and length/CRC fields whose comparisons against libpng's expected literals pass, so deeper chunk-parsing populates info_ptr arrays and png_free_data is later reached with num != -1.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry intact chunk headers (PNG/IHDR/gAMA/sRGB at fixed offsets) and the winner-only coverage reaches per-chunk free paths (tRNS 508, eXIf 621, hIST 641, PLTE 650) that the loser never enters, indicating more chunks parsed to valid state.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds corrupt the chunk-type / length / CRC region (e.g. offsets 0x1d-0x1e, 0x2c-0x3d differ with no concentration toward the valid literals), so those chunk comparisons fail and the per-item free branches stay un-hit (loser L=0 on the blocker true side).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads intercepted integer/string CMP operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected chunk-type/CRC literal directly into the matching input offset, letting cmplog clear the chunk-validation comparisons that naive must hit by chance; verified by an I2S-floor dash-row ancestor in the winner lineage.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of if (num != -1) at png.c:674 inside png_free_data, globally blocked on side T.
cmplog wins over naive with full divergence at this branch via the I2S technique delta.
value_profile wins over naive with full divergence at this branch via the value_profile technique delta.
Winner-only coverage enters per-chunk free paths (tRNS/eXIf/hIST/PLTE) that the loser never reaches, evidencing deeper chunk parsing.
Winner seeds carry intact PNG/IHDR/gAMA/sRGB chunk headers.
Loser-blocking seeds differ from winners at chunk-field offsets with no concentration toward the valid literals.
The loser is blocked on the true side for the full campaign while the winner resolves it.
value_profile accumulates a positive hitcount on the branch while the loser stays at zero.

An input that produces a

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which all chunk-validation comparisons are removed (chunks accepted unconditionally) but the num != -1 branch still resolved far more often under cmplog/value_profile than naive — showing the divergence is not driven by comparison-gated chunk parsing.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact offsets whose comparisons gate reaching num != -1 are inferred from the per-role coverage and PNG chunk layout, not pinned to a single concentrated I2S-substitutable byte run in the BYTE DIFF (all rows are PARTIAL).

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts chunk-type and header-field comparisons recorded when libpng validates the tRNS chunk, substitutes expected operands at matching header offsets, deterministically constructing the accepted tRNS-bearing image that makes input_has_transparency nonzero and the false branch reachable.
signature.one_line: I2S constructs valid tRNS chunk structure so transparency flag becomes nonzero

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a parseable tRNS chunk so png_ptr->num_trans>0, making input_has_transparency nonzero and taking the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid transparency (tRNS) chunk that libpng's chunk dispatcher accepts so that png_ptr->num_trans > 0; this requires the chunk header bytes (length, the 4-byte 'tRNS' FOURCC and consistent CRC/structure) plus an IHDR whose color_type/bit_depth are accepted, so input_has_transparency = png_ptr->num_trans > 0 evaluates nonzero and line 1227 takes the false side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving cmplog seeds carry concentrated, structurally-consistent header bytes (e.g. IHDR color_type 0x18=08 and bit_depth-area 0x19=02 fixed across all 10 winners, and 0x1d=52 0x1e=ed fixed) that produce an accepted image with a tRNS chunk, so num_trans>0 and the branch falls through to line 1228 which the loser never reaches.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive seeds scatter exactly those header offsets (0x18 spreads over 04/10/02/01, 0x19=00 in all 10, 0x1d over 00/7f) so the chunk/image is rejected before a tRNS is parsed; num_trans stays 0, input_has_transparency==0 is true, and the false branch is never taken in any of 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operands captured when libpng compares the parsed chunk-type / header fields against the literals it expects, then substitutes the expected operand into the input at the matching offset, deterministically constructing the accepted tRNS-bearing header that naive's blind havoc cannot stumble onto in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked false side of line 1227 is gated on input_has_transparency, which is png_ptr->num_trans > 0.
The branch under analysis is if (input_has_transparency == 0) and its false side is the globally blocked side.
Only winner seeds reach line 1228 (the false-branch body), the loser never does.
Winner takes the false side in all 10 trials while loser takes the true side in all 10 trials.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1227  T=0 F=10  T=10 F=0  if (input_has_transparency == 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner header byte offset 0x18 is fixed at 08 across all 10 winners but scattered in losers.
Winner byte at 0x19 is fixed at 02 while every loser has 00 there.
Winner bytes at 0x1d/0x1e are concentrated (52/ed) while losers diverge there.
cmplog augments naive with I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for a matching operand and substitutes the other operand from intercepted CMPs.

scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly confirm from the prompt bytes that a complete tRNS chunk is what sets num_trans>0; the concentrated winner bytes are in the IHDR region (0x18-0x1e) and the inferred tRNS-acceptance chain rests on libpng semantics rather than an explicit tRNS-offset byte diff.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes chunk-type FOURCCs and structural field comparands logged during PNG chunk parsing, allowing cmplog to construct a valid tRNS chunk and RGB color_type that populates num_trans; naive cannot assemble this multi-constraint structure by random mutation.
signature.one_line: I2S builds valid tRNS chunk enabling num_trans!=0 nonzero presence check

analysis.summary_one_line: A valid non-palette PNG carrying a tRNS chunk (num_trans!=0) under the PNG_EXPAND transformation, requiring exact chunk-type/length bytes.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid PNG that parses past IHDR into a non-palette (RGB, color_type=2) image and carries a tRNS chunk so that png_ptr->num_trans becomes non-zero, reaching the true side of line 1967 inside the else (non-palette) arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds are cmplog-discovered, well-formed PNGs (correct magic 89 50 4e 47 0d 0a 1a 0a, IHDR, gAMA, sRGB chunks) with IHDR color_type byte = 02 (RGB) held at offset 0x19 across all 10 winners, routing into the else branch; the blocker true side fired T=10 F=0 for W meaning num_trans was set non-zero by a successfully-parsed tRNS chunk.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser (naive) seeds at offset 0x19 carry 00 (x8) and 04 (x2) instead of the winner's 02 RGB color_type, and corrupt the structural bytes (e.g. offset 0x1d shows R=52 in all winners but 7f/00/ff in losers), so parsing never establishes a non-palette image with a valid tRNS chunk; the blocker fired T=0 F=10 for L (num_trans stayed 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact comparand operands (PNG chunk-type FOURCCs and length/CRC fields compared during chunk parsing) into the input at the matching byte offsets, letting cmplog construct a valid tRNS chunk and the RGB color_type that naive's blind havoc mutators cannot reliably reproduce.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the true side of `if (png_ptr->num_trans != 0)`, gating on a tRNS chunk having set num_trans.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff evidence ties the divergence to chunk-structure and color_type bytes, but it does not pin the specific tRNS chunk-type/length comparand that I2S substitutes, so the exact CMP operand driving the win is inferred rather than directly observed.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads CmpLogObserver and substitutes chunk-name and header comparands from libpng's chunk-parsing CMPs, repairing the structural layout needed for a valid tRNS chunk; naive's blind havoc never satisfies these multi-byte structural constraints, leaving num_trans==0.
signature.one_line: I2S repairs chunk structural bytes to satisfy tRNS parsing and PNG_EXPAND_tRNS flag

analysis.summary_one_line: Input needs a valid tRNS chunk so num_trans!=0 and PNG_EXPAND_tRNS is set for a non-palette color type.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG whose IHDR declares a non-palette color type (so the else branch at 4790 is taken) plus a structurally valid tRNS chunk that makes png_ptr->num_trans != 0 AND sets the PNG_EXPAND_tRNS transformation. This depends on chunk type bytes and length fields being correct enough for libpng to parse the chunk and accept the transparency, not just a single magic literal.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog winner seeds keep the chunk/header byte region intact and consistent (offsets 0x35-0x3d are uniformly 73 52 47 42 ... 'sRGB' across all 10 W seeds, and IHDR color-type byte 0x19 is uniformly 02 = RGB, a non-palette type), preserving a parseable chunk layout that lets the tRNS chunk register so num_trans!=0 and PNG_EXPAND_tRNS fires; W is the only role that ever takes the true side of the PNG_EXPAND_tRNS operand at L4793.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds corrupt the IHDR/chunk bytes (offset 0x19 holds 00 in 8 of 10 L seeds rather than the uniform 02 of W, and the chunk-name region 0x35-0x3d carries stray non-'sRGB' bytes like 74/45/58 'tEXt'-style), so the tRNS condition never holds: every L seed takes the false branch at L4792 and L4793 is never reached as true.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand into the input wherever the other operand's bytes appear, repairing the chunk-name / header comparands that libpng tests while parsing chunks; naive can only stumble on these multi-byte structural constraints by chance havoc, which never lands in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the tRNS expansion guard requiring num_trans!=0 and PNG_EXPAND_tRNS in the non-palette else arm.
The second operand of the gate is the PNG_EXPAND_tRNS transformation flag.
Only the winner role ever takes the true side of the PNG_EXPAND_tRNS operand at L4793; the loser never reaches it true.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4793  T=181 F=0  T=0 F=0  (png_ptr->transformations & PNG_EXPAND_tRNS) != 0)

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds hold a uniform intact chunk-name region (sRGB) at offsets 0x35-0x3d while loser seeds carry stray non-sRGB bytes there.
Winner seeds have a uniform non-palette color-type byte 02 at offset 0x19 while loser seeds mostly hold 00 there.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a single fixed multi-byte tRNS magic literal where naive resolves the branch about as often as cmplog within the same budget, showing the structural chunk constraint is not what I2S substitution is solving.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff shows multi-byte structural divergence (color-type and chunk-name regions) rather than one clean concentrated comparand, so the exact CMP operand I2S substitutes to satisfy num_trans!=0/PNG_EXPAND_tRNS is inferred from chunk-parsing semantics, not pinned to a specific offset.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the compression-info equality comparand (compression-flag and compression-type bytes) so the iTXt chunk passes the L2777-2779 gate, enabling the third NUL-scan loop at L2796 to execute and exit normally.
signature.one_line: I2S satisfies compression-info gate bytes to reach inner loop

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed iTXt chunk whose translated-keyword field starts with a non-zero byte before length-end, so the L2796 NUL-scan loop runs and exits false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An iTXt chunk that survives the compression-info gate at buffer[prefix_length+1] (==0 or (==1 && buffer[prefix_length+2]==PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE)) so control reaches the third loop at L2796; the translated-keyword region must contain at least one non-zero byte within `length` so the loop body executes and the false (loop-exit) side is taken at L2796.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog reaches the L2796 loop and exits it (avg hitcount on branch winner=23, resolved 10/10); I2SRandReplace can substitute the byte values the compression-info comparison expects (buffer[prefix_length+2]==PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE) so the chunk passes the L2777-2779 gate, letting the third NUL-scan loop run to completion.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 with avg hitcount on branch loser=0 and stays blocked 9/10; its random havoc rarely hits the exact compression-info byte the gate compares against, so control diverts to the L2853 'bad compression info' else and never reaches L2796.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the equality CMP guarding the iTXt compression-info gate and splices the logged comparand into the input at the matching offset, manufacturing a gate-passing chunk that naive's blind havoc cannot reliably produce.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false (loop-exit) side of the third NUL-scan loop over the translated-keyword field at pngrutil.c:2796.
Reaching L2796 requires passing the compression-info gate that compares buffer[prefix_length+2] against PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE.
Inputs that fail the compression-info gate divert to the bad-compression-info else and never reach L2796.
cmplog wins over naive at this branch with prob_div 0.90 and a large hitcount divergence on the branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=23  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: naive resolves only 1/10 and stays blocked 9/10 at this branch.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CMP buffer and substitutes one operand at input offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins over naive at this branch via the ctx_coverage technique with prob_div 0.80.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so a new (call-context, edge) pair counts as new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 at this branch with positive on-branch hitcount.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=6  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: naive's feedback is a bare per-edge hit-count bucket with no context indexing and no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic iTXt-handler harness where the compression-info gate byte is fixed/constant (no input-derived equality CMP) shows cmplog NOT outperforming naive at reaching the L2796 loop-exit, indicating I2S substitution was not the enabling factor.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the L2777-2779 compression-info equality is the specific CMP that I2SRandReplace solves is inferred from source structure; the lineage dash row confirms an I2S find in the chain but does not prove it targeted that exact comparand.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace satisfies the compression-flag/type equality CMPs at L2777-2779 so the iTXt chunk enters the inner block, then lands on the false (compressed==0 or truncated) side of L2810 that naive's havoc almost never produces.
signature.one_line: I2S satisfies compression-flag gate, letting false side of L2810 fire

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed iTXt chunk whose compression-flag/type bytes pass the dispatch but whose compressed/prefix_length combo fails the compressed-branch test.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An iTXt chunk where the compression-flag byte at buffer[prefix_length+1] is 0 (or 1 with a compression-type byte equal to PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE), so the chunk enters the inner block at L2779, but is NOT (compressed != 0 AND prefix_length < length) — i.e. it takes the false side of L2810 via the L2807 compressed==0 path or the trailing else 'truncated'.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog resolves 10/10; its winning seeds carry concrete iTXt-chunk bytes (PNG magic + IHDR + gAMA + sRGB) and the byte-diff shows the compression-flag region at offset 0x2e holds fc (the chunk passes the L2779 dispatch but lands on the compressed==0 / truncated false side of the blocker at L2810).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 and is blocked 9/10 averaging 20.30h; the divergent-branch view shows naive (L) takes the TRUE side of L2810 (T=10 F=0) — its inputs satisfy compressed!=0 && prefix_length<length and never produce the false-side compression-flag/length combination.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the byte sequence matching one operand of the equality CMPs at L2777-2779 (compression-flag == 0/1, compression-type == PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE) and substitutes the satisfying operand directly into the input, letting it pass the dispatch gate and reach the false side of the blocker that naive's blind havoc almost never hits.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of an else-if testing compressed!=0 && prefix_length<length in the iTXt handler.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
Reaching the blocker requires passing the compression-flag/type dispatch at L2777-2779.
The false side of the blocker is taken via the compressed==0 path at L2807 or the trailing else 'truncated'.
cmplog resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive is blocked in 9.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: The loser (naive) takes the TRUE side of the blocker and never the false side that resolves it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2810  T=0 F=27  T=10 F=0  else if (compressed != 0 && prefix_length < length)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The compression-flag region byte differs between winner and loser at offset 0x2e.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans for one CMP operand in the input and substitutes the other.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seeds are stored by naive_ctx and carry the PNG/iTXt chunk byte layout.
naive_ctx resolves 9/10 vs naive 1/10 with much shorter blocked time.
naive_ctx folds call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is a new coverage bucket per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive uses a bare per-edge hit-count bucket with no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic iTXt-style harness whose only roadblock is the equality dispatch on the compression-flag/type bytes shows cmplog NOT beating naive at reaching the post-dispatch false side, indicating the divergence is not driven by I2S solving those equality CMPs.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff at offset 0x2e (fc vs 05) is attributed to the compression-flag/type region by structural inference; the exact mapping of file offset 0x2e to buffer[prefix_length+1] inside the iTXt chunk is not directly proven by the prompt.
